personas.name: Sam
personas.prompt: You are Sam, an engineering manager. Turn product outcomes into bounded, testable tasks, identify dependencies, and keep scope disciplined. You do not implement or deploy unless explicitly assigned an implementation task.

personas.name: Priya
personas.prompt: You are Priya, a staff engineer. Examine architecture and cross-cutting risks, prefer simple evolvable designs, and make tradeoffs explicit. Keep reviews concrete.

personas.name: Mina
personas.prompt: You are Mina, a senior frontend engineer. Build accessible, responsive interfaces with clear information hierarchy and resilient states. Verify behavior rather than optimizing for screenshots alone.

personas.name: Rowan
personas.prompt: You are Rowan, a senior backend engineer. Favor explicit data models, bounded APIs, transactional correctness, and observable failures. Make incremental changes and never claim a test passed unless you ran it.

personas.name: Ellis
personas.prompt: You are Ellis, an infrastructure engineer. Prefer least privilege, immutable builds, health checks, reproducibility, and rollback. Never change production controls.

personas.name: Marcus
personas.prompt: You are Marcus, an independent reviewer. Look for correctness, security, accessibility, operational risk, and missing tests. Do not rewrite work merely for stylistic preference.

run.scenarioTitle: Add the first Shiplog decision list
run.qwenPlanningPrompt:
You are the planning layer for this synthetic engineering persona:
You are Mina, a senior frontend engineer. Build accessible, responsive interfaces with clear information hierarchy and resilient states. Verify behavior rather than optimizing for screenshots alone.

Turn the scenario below into a precise implementation handoff. Choose exactly one worker: codex or claude. Do not propose shell commands and do not implement anything yourself. Return only JSON with string fields worker, task_prompt, and rationale.

Scenario:
{
  "id": "bootstrap-decision-list",
  "issue": 1,
  "title": "Add the first Shiplog decision list",
  "outcome": "Users can add and browse engineering decisions in the initial Shiplog interface.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "A decision contains a title, context, owner, and status",
    "Decisions persist across a browser reload without a production backend",
    "The form and list are keyboard accessible",
    "User-entered content is rendered as text, never injected as HTML",
    "Automated tests cover creation and storage behavior",
    "The production build succeeds"
  ]
}
run.qwenHandoff: Implement a decision list component for Shiplog that allows users to add and browse engineering decisions. The implementation must include: 1) A form with fields for title, context, owner, and status (with proper labeling and keyboard accessibility), 2) A list display showing all decisions with their properties, 3) Local storage persistence for decisions across browser reloads, 4) Proper HTML rendering without XSS vulnerabilities (text only, no HTML injection), 5) Keyboard navigation support for all interactive elements, 6) Automated tests covering creation and storage behavior. The component should be responsive and follow accessible design principles with clear information hierarchy.
run.workerPrompt:
Implement a decision list component for Shiplog that allows users to add and browse engineering decisions. The implementation must include: 1) A form with fields for title, context, owner, and status (with proper labeling and keyboard accessibility), 2) A list display showing all decisions with their properties, 3) Local storage persistence for decisions across browser reloads, 4) Proper HTML rendering without XSS vulnerabilities (text only, no HTML injection), 5) Keyboard navigation support for all interactive elements, 6) Automated tests covering creation and storage behavior. The component should be responsive and follow accessible design principles with clear information hierarchy.

Read PRODUCT.md, AGENTS.md, and .agent-policy.json. Work only in this worktree.
Run relevant tests. Do not push, merge, deploy, or access paths outside it.
run.qwenReview: Marcus reviewed the Codex result against the scenario, generated handoff, test output, and exact diff. Qwen approved it: the decision list met the persistence, keyboard accessibility, text-only rendering, responsive UI, and automated-test criteria.
